class.name: CollisionObject3D
class.brief_description: Abstract base class for 3D physics objects.
class.description:
Abstract base class for 3D physics objects. [CollisionObject3D] can hold any number of [Shape3D]s for collision. Each shape must be assigned to a [i]shape owner[/i]. Shape owners are not nodes and do not appear in the editor, but are accessible through code using the [code]shape_owner_*[/code] methods.
[b]Damage System:[/b] CollisionObject3D includes a comprehensive damage system similar to Unreal Engine's damage framework. It supports multiple damage types, damage events, immunity, multipliers, and helper methods for common damage scenarios like radial damage and knockback.
[b]Warning:[/b] With a non-uniform scale, this node will likely not behave as expected. It is advised to keep its scale the same on all axes and adjust its collision shape(s) instead.

method.name: _input_event
method.description: Receives unhandled [InputEvent]s. [param event_position] is the location in world space of the mouse pointer on the surface of the shape with index [param shape_idx] and [param normal] is the normal vector of the surface at that point. Connect to the [signal input_event] signal to easily pick up these events. [b]Note:[/b] [method _input_event] requires [member input_ray_pickable] to be [code]true[/code] and at least one [member collision_layer] bit to be set.

method.name: _mouse_enter
method.description: Called when the mouse pointer enters any of this object's shapes. Requires [member input_ray_pickable] to be [code]true[/code] and at least one [member collision_layer] bit to be set. Note that moving between different shapes within a single [CollisionObject3D] won't cause this function to be called.

method.name: _mouse_exit
method.description: Called when the mouse pointer exits all this object's shapes. Requires [member input_ray_pickable] to be [code]true[/code] and at least one [member collision_layer] bit to be set. Note that moving between different shapes within a single [CollisionObject3D] won't cause this function to be called.

method.name: create_shape_owner
method.description: Creates a new shape owner for the given object. Returns [code]owner_id[/code] of the new owner for future reference.

method.name: get_collision_layer_value
method.description: Returns whether or not the specified layer of the [member collision_layer] is enabled, given a [param layer_number] between 1 and 32.

method.name: get_collision_mask_value
method.description: Returns whether or not the specified layer of the [member collision_mask] is enabled, given a [param layer_number] between 1 and 32.

method.name: get_rid
method.description: Returns the object's [RID].

method.name: get_shape_owners
method.description: Returns an [Array] of [code]owner_id[/code] identifiers. You can use these ids in other methods that take [code]owner_id[/code] as an argument.

method.name: is_shape_owner_disabled
method.description: If [code]true[/code], the shape owner and its shapes are disabled.

method.name: remove_shape_owner
method.description: Removes the given shape owner.

method.name: set_collision_layer_value
method.description: Based on [param value], enables or disables the specified layer in the [member collision_layer], given a [param layer_number] between 1 and 32.

method.name: set_collision_mask_value
method.description: Based on [param value], enables or disables the specified layer in the [member collision_mask], given a [param layer_number] between 1 and 32.

method.name: shape_find_owner
method.description: Returns the [code]owner_id[/code] of the given shape.

method.name: shape_owner_add_shape
method.description: Adds a [Shape3D] to the shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_clear_shapes
method.description: Removes all shapes from the shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_get_owner
method.description: Returns the parent object of the given shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_get_shape
method.description: Returns the [Shape3D] with the given ID from the given shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_get_shape_count
method.description: Returns the number of shapes the given shape owner contains.

method.name: shape_owner_get_shape_index
method.description: Returns the child index of the [Shape3D] with the given ID from the given shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_get_transform
method.description: Returns the shape owner's [Transform3D].

method.name: shape_owner_remove_shape
method.description: Removes a shape from the given shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_set_disabled
method.description: If [code]true[/code], disables the given shape owner.

method.name: shape_owner_set_transform
method.description: Sets the [Transform3D] of the given shape owner.

method.name: apply_damage_simple
method.description: Applies damage to this collision object. Returns the actual damage dealt after immunity and multipliers are applied. [param damage_types] is a bitmask of [enum DamageType] values. [param instigator] is the object that caused the damage, and [param damage_causer] is the specific object that inflicted it (e.g., a weapon).

method.name: can_receive_damage
method.description: Returns whether this object can receive damage of the specified types. If [param damage_types] is -1, checks if any damage can be received.

method.name: set_damage_immunity
method.description: Sets immunity status for the specified damage types. [param damage_types] is a bitmask of [enum DamageType] values.

method.name: has_damage_immunity
method.description: Returns whether this object has immunity to the specified damage types.

method.name: get_damage_multiplier
method.description: Returns the damage multiplier for the specified damage types. Default is 1.0.

method.name: set_damage_multiplier
method.description: Sets the damage multiplier for the specified damage types. Values below 1.0 reduce damage, values above 1.0 increase damage.

method.name: deal_damage_to
method.description: Deals damage to a target collision object. Returns the actual damage dealt.

method.name: deal_radial_damage
method.description:
Deals radial damage in a sphere around the specified center. Returns total damage dealt to all targets. If [param falloff] is true, damage decreases with distance from center. [param falloff_type] determines the falloff curve type (see [enum RadialFalloffType]). [param falloff_curve] adjusts the intensity of the falloff curve.
[b]Falloff Types:[/b] - [constant FALLOFF_LINEAR]: Linear decrease (1.0 - distance/radius) - [constant FALLOFF_QUADRATIC]: Quadratic decrease (1.0 - (distance/radius)²) - [constant FALLOFF_EXPONENTIAL]: Exponential decrease (e^(-curve * distance/radius)) - [constant FALLOFF_INVERSE_SQUARE]: Inverse square law (1/(1 + curve * (distance/radius)²)) - [constant FALLOFF_STEP]: Step function with [param falloff_curve] steps - [constant FALLOFF_CURVED]: Power curve ((1 - distance/radius)^curve)

method.name: apply_knockback
method.description: Applies knockback force in the specified direction. Returns the knockback strength applied. Base implementation returns the strength value. Override in subclasses for actual physics.

method.name: create_damage_event
method.description: Creates a damage event dictionary with the specified parameters. Useful for creating complex damage events.

method.name: _handle_damage
method.description: Virtual method called when damage is being processed. Override to implement custom damage handling logic. Return the processed damage amount, or modify the damage_event dictionary to change the damage.

method.name: _on_damage_received
method.description: Virtual method called when this object receives damage. Override to implement custom damage response logic.

method.name: _on_damage_dealt
method.description: Virtual method called when this object deals damage to another object. Override to implement custom damage dealing logic.

members.member: The physics layers this CollisionObject3D [b]is in[/b]. Collision objects can exist in one or more of 32 different layers. See also [member collision_mask]. [b]Note:[/b] Object A can detect a contact with object B only if object B is in any of the layers that object A scans. See [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/physics/physics_introduction.html#collision-layers-and-masks]Collision layers and masks[/url] in the documentation for more information.

The physics layers this CollisionObject3D [b]scans[/b]. Collision objects can scan one or more of 32 different layers. See also [member collision_layer]. [b]Note:[/b] Object A can detect a contact with object B only if object B is in any of the layers that object A scans. See [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/physics/physics_introduction.html#collision-layers-and-masks]Collision layers and masks[/url] in the documentation for more information.

The priority used to solve colliding when occurring penetration. The higher the priority is, the lower the penetration into the object will be. This can for example be used to prevent the player from breaking through the boundaries of a level.
Defines the behavior in physics when [member Node.process_mode] is set to [constant Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED].
If [code]true[/code], the [CollisionObject3D] will continue to receive input events as the mouse is dragged across its shapes.

If [code]true[/code], this object is pickable. A pickable object can detect the mouse pointer entering/leaving, and if the mouse is inside it, report input events. Requires at least one [member collision_layer] bit to be set.

signal.name: input_event
signal.description: Emitted when the object receives an unhandled [InputEvent]. [param event_position] is the location in world space of the mouse pointer on the surface of the shape with index [param shape_idx] and [param normal] is the normal vector of the surface at that point.

signal.name: mouse_entered
signal.description: Emitted when the mouse pointer enters any of this object's shapes. Requires [member input_ray_pickable] to be [code]true[/code] and at least one [member collision_layer] bit to be set. [b]Note:[/b] Due to the lack of continuous collision detection, this signal may not be emitted in the expected order if the mouse moves fast enough and the [CollisionObject3D]'s area is small. This signal may also not be emitted if another [CollisionObject3D] is overlapping the [CollisionObject3D] in question.

signal.name: mouse_exited
signal.description: Emitted when the mouse pointer exits all this object's shapes. Requires [member input_ray_pickable] to be [code]true[/code] and at least one [member collision_layer] bit to be set. [b]Note:[/b] Due to the lack of continuous collision detection, this signal may not be emitted in the expected order if the mouse moves fast enough and the [CollisionObject3D]'s area is small. This signal may also not be emitted if another [CollisionObject3D] is overlapping the [CollisionObject3D] in question.

signal.name: damage_received
signal.description: Emitted when this object receives damage. [param damage_event] contains detailed damage information, [param actual_damage] is the damage after immunity and multipliers.

signal.name: damage_dealt
signal.description: Emitted when this object deals damage to another object. [param damage_event] contains detailed damage information, [param actual_damage] is the damage dealt.

signal.name: damage_applied
signal.description: Emitted when damage is applied to a target. [param target] is the object that received damage, [param damage_event] contains detailed damage information, [param actual_damage] is the damage dealt.

signal.name: recieve_damage
signal.description: [b]Legacy signal[/b] for backward compatibility. Emitted when damage is received. Use [signal damage_received] for new code.

constants.constant: When [member Node.process_mode] is set to [constant Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED], remove from the physics simulation to stop all physics interactions with this [CollisionObject3D]. Automatically re-added to the physics simulation when the [Node] is processed again.

When [member Node.process_mode] is set to [constant Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED], make the body static. Doesn't affect [Area3D]. [PhysicsBody3D] can't be affected by forces or other bodies while static. Automatically set [PhysicsBody3D] back to its original mode when the [Node] is processed again.
When [member Node.process_mode] is set to [constant Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED], do not affect the physics simulation.
Generic damage type, used as default.
Physical damage from impacts, blunt force, etc.
Fire damage from flames, heat, etc.
Electric damage from electricity, lightning, etc.
Poison damage from toxins, damage over time, etc.
Explosive damage from explosions, blasts, etc.
Melee damage from close combat attacks.
Ranged damage from projectiles, bullets, etc.
Magical damage from spells, enchantments, etc.
Radial damage from area of effect attacks.
Environmental damage from hazards, traps, etc.
Linear falloff: damage decreases linearly with distance.
Quadratic falloff: damage decreases with the square of distance.
Exponential falloff: damage decreases exponentially with distance.
Inverse square falloff: follows physics-like inverse square law.
Step falloff: damage decreases in discrete steps.
Curved falloff: customizable power curve falloff.
